project_info.title: 0.01 Degree
project_info.core_selling_points: One line of code saves humanity; 0.01 degree reversal twist; morally complex eco-terrorists
project_info.antagonist_tiers: 小:Joe/Dr.Freeman, 中:Mysterious Man, 大:Group Leader(TBD)
project_info.factions: Mars Government, SpaceNano Corp, National Energy Lab, Radical Animal Rights Group, Black Market
project_info.gf_irreversible_cost: If caught, Peter dies; one chance only
project_info.target_reader: Sci-fi readers interested in time travel, moral philosophy, tech thrillers

active_threads: Particle accelerator acquisition via Dr. Freeman
Three technologies converging on Kuiper Belt shuttle
foreshadowing: Marcus Webb files regulatory inquiry about accelerator purchase (ch3) - delayed alarm
Kepler-7 can generate micro black holes, 7ms duration (ch3) - capability reveal
Freeman's organization has ship preparing for outer solar system (ch3) - Kuiper Belt connection
Office of Technology Regulation inspector dispatched to Building 7 — same office processing Marcus Webb's accelerator inquiry (ch4) - threads may cross
Job listing posted for planetary navigation programmer with oddly specific qualifications (ch4) - Peter's recruitment hook
Three operatives (Steve/Joe, Freeman, Dr. Park) each believe they are the only one (ch4) - compartmentalized conspiracy

hook.content: Marcus files regulatory inquiry but it will take weeks; Freeman is already preparing a ship to the outer solar system. The accelerator will be long gone.

ending.location: Prometheus Facility / transport pod leaving Olympus City

hook.content: Three stolen technologies loaded onto one shuttle for the Kuiper Belt. A job listing appears for a planetary navigation programmer — the final piece has no idea what he is walking into.

ending.location: Unmarked launch pad, outskirts of Olympus City / Mars (implied Peter location)

history.note: Accelerator acquisition thread advanced - Freeman secures Kepler-7
Sun 2.0 theft completes third acquisition; all three pieces converge on single shuttle; Peter recruitment hook drops — constellation of plot threads crystallizes
